Document:
# Slimeperson
[bold]Slimepeople[/bold] are a [bold][color=#f44]Nitrogen-breathing[/color][/bold], [bold][color=#6f0]Gelatinous[/color][/bold] species; with rubbery membranes keeping squishy organs suspended in goo.
Slimepeople have an [bold]innate storage grid[/bold] in their body, that they can keep small objects in. However, [bold]anyone can see inside[/bold] and take the items out [bold]without asking[/bold] at any moment!
Slimepeople have the baseline body temperature of [bold][color=yellow]37°C[/color][/bold] (~[color=yellow]310°K[/color]). They experience the effects of [bold]hypothermia at [color=#4df]-13°C[/color][/bold] and [bold]hyperthermia at [color=#f44]52°C[/color][/bold]. (~[color=#4df]260°K[/color] & [color=#f44]325°K[/color])
Slimepeople are [bold]omnivorous[/bold], meaning they can digest [bold]meat meat and plant based foods[/bold], but, they [bold]can't stomach raw meat or blood[/bold].
Their bodies can process [bold]6 reagents[/bold] at a time.
Slimepeople [bold]passively regenerate[/bold] at [bold]roughly doule the rate[/bold] compared to most species, all types of [bold][color=yellow]Brute[/color]damage [/bold], and individually [bold][color=#f44]Heat[/color]damage [/bold].
Slimeperson names typically follow a "[bold]Firstname Lastname[/bold]" pattern, with frequent emphasis on plosive [color=#999](/p, b/)[/color] sounds, sometimes resembling ontomanopea.
